tool.name: Kraken OCR
tool.description: extract text from segmented or line document images
tool.command:
#if $output_format_flag == '-h' #set $out_ext = 'html' #elif $output_format_flag == '-n' #set $out_ext = 'txt' #else #set $out_ext = 'xml' #end if @REJECT_MULTIPAGE_TIFF_BASED_ON_FILE_METADATA@
ln -s '$input_type.image' 'input.${input_type.image.ext}' && #if $input_type.source == 'segmented' #set $segmentation_ext = 'xml' ln -s '$input_type.segmentation' 'segmentation.${segmentation_ext}' && #end if
#if $input_type.image.ext != 'pdf' ln -s '$output' 'kraken_output.${out_ext}' && #end if
#if $model_source.source == 'history' #set $model_name = str($model_source.model.name) ln -s '$model_source.model' '$model_name' && #end if
@RUN_KRAKEN_OCR@ #if $model_source.source == 'datatable' --model '$model' #else --model '$model_name' #end if --pad '$pad' --temperature '$temperature' $reordering.mode #if $reordering.mode == '--reorder' --base-dir '$reordering.base_dir' #end if #if $input_type.source == 'line' --no-segmentation #end if --text-direction '$text_direction' #if $no_legacy_polygons --no-legacy-polygons #end if

param.name: source
param.label: Input type
param.help: Select whether the input is a pre-segmented image with text regions or a single line image

param.name: segmentation
param.label: Segmentation XML
param.help: File containing pre-segmented text regions in ALTO or PAGE format

param.name: source
param.label: Model source
param.help: Select a model from the list or upload your own model file.

param.name: model
param.label: OCR model
param.help: Optical character recognition model used by Kraken.

param.name: model
param.label: OCR model
param.help: Kraken OCR model file from your history.

param.label: Padding to add around line images for OCR
param.help: Increase it to give the model more margin around tightly cropped text; decrease it to add less extra whitespace. This reduces the image area processed by OCR.

param.label: Softmax temperature
param.help: Control how sharply Kraken converts model scores into character probabilities. Values below 1.0 produce sharper confidence scores, while values above 1.0 produce flatter confidence scores. The recognized text usually remains unchanged.

param.name: mode
param.label: Output text in logical order using the Unicode BiDi algorithm
param.help: Choose whether Kraken should apply the Unicode bidirectional (BiDi) text algorithm to produce OCR output in logical text order. This is mainly relevant for right-to-left text, or mixed left-to-right and right-to-left text.

param.label: Initial text direction for BiDi reordering
param.help: Choose the initial text direction used when Kraken applies the Unicode BiDi algorithm. Auto allows explicit text direction information in ALTO/PAGE XML input to take precedence when available. For single-line image input, or when no text direction is present in the XML input, select Left-to-right or Right-to-left as appropriate for the OCR model and text.

param.label: OCR text direction
param.help: Select Horizontal, top-to-bottom for normal horizontal text lines such as English, German, Greek, Arabic, or Hebrew; Vertical, left-to-right for vertical text with columns ordered left-to-right; or Vertical, right-to-left for vertical text with columns ordered right-to-left, such as traditional Chinese, Japanese, or Korean layouts.

param.name: no_legacy_polygons
param.label: Disable legacy polygon output
param.help: Choose whether to support legacy polygon formats in segmentation input. Enable it for compatibility with older segmentation files.

has_text.text: multi-page TIF(F) input is not supported

tool.help:
**What it does**
kraken is an open-source, turn-key automatic text recognition (ATR) system optimized for historical and non-Latin script writing. This tool only exposes Kraken's OCR command.
OCR is the process of extracting text from images. Single-line images can be processed directly. For document images, a segmentation file in [ALTO](https://www.loc.gov/standards/alto/) or [PAGE XML](https://github.com/PRImA-Research-Lab/PAGE-XML/wiki) format is required. This additional input file can be produced by the Kraken segmentation tool, which is also available in the Kraken OCR suite.
The OCR output can be returned in various formats, including [ALTO](https://www.loc.gov/standards/alto/), [PageXML](https://github.com/PRImA-Research-Lab/PAGE-XML/wiki), [hOCR](https://kba.github.io/hocr-spec/1.2/), [abbyyXML](https://support.abbyy.com/hc/en-us/articles/360017270080-Output-XML-document), and plain text.
**License**
Kraken is licensed under the Apache-2.0 license.
